abstract: Urban traffic congestion has become a pervasive challenge, leading to significant fuel waste, economic productivity loss, and delays in emergency response. Traditional traffic management systems primarily rely on Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) using static round-robin scheduling, which fail to adapt to real-time demands. While recent IoT-based solutions have attempted to use traffic density for signal control, their performance is often hindered by a lack of diverse emergency vehicle datasets for training deep neural networks. In this paper, we present a novel distributed IoT framework built upon two key observations. First, recognizing that road infrastructure remains largely static, we develop a two-stage vehicle detector that leverages background subtraction and deep learning to achieve 77% detection accuracy on the UA-DETRAC dataset. Second, we address the identification of emergency vehicles by focusing on their unique acoustic signatures. By implementing a novel siren detection algorithm on edge devices, our system achieves an exceptional emergency vehicle detection accuracy of 99.4%. The integration of these two approaches provides a robust, real-time solution for intelligent traffic management and priority routing.
